'''Mark Ioane''' (born 3 December 1990) is a New Zealand professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] and [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and the [[London Broncos]] and [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Super League]].

==Early years==
Ioane was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand. He attended [[De La Salle College, Mangere East|De La Salle College]] in [[Māngere East]].

==Playing career==
===New Zealand Warriors===
Ioane was signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and played 25 games for the Junior Warriors in the [[NRL Under-20s|2010 Toyota Cup]], scoring four tries.<ref>{{cite web|date=2010|title=IOANE, MARK (2010)|url=http://www.nzleague.co.nz/pages/warriors_players_U20.php?player=58&name=IOANE,%20Mark|url-status=dead|access-date=2 July 2015|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130208052245/http://nzleague.co.nz/pages/warriors_players_U20.php?player=58&name=IOANE%2C%20Mark|archivedate=8 February 2013}}</ref> He started at prop in the grand final, won by the Junior Warriors 42-28.

He was selected by the [[Junior Kiwis]] at the end of 2010.

===Canberra Raiders===
In 2011 Ioane joined the [[Canberra Raiders]] but could not break into the first grade side. While at the Raiders Ioane played for the [[Souths Logan Magpies]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] and the [[Mount Pritchard Mounties]] in the [[NSW Cup]]. Ioane won the Mounties player of the year award in 2012.<ref>{{cite web|last=Lucas|first=Jack|date=8 November 2012|title=Mark Ioane holding nothing back for NRL dream|url=http://www.titans.com.au/news-display/Mark-Ioane-holding-nothing-back-for-NRL-dream/65741|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130514145143/http://www.titans.com.au/news-display/Mark-Ioane-holding-nothing-back-for-NRL-dream/65741|archive-date=14 May 2013|access-date=3 June 2021}}</ref>

===Gold Coast Titans===
Ioane signed for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in 2013 on a one season deal and played for the [[Burleigh Bears]] in the Queensland Cup.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.goldcoast.com.au/article/2012/11/09/441493_gold-coast-titans.html|title=NRL 2013 and Rugby League News | Gold Coast Bulletin|publisher=Goldcoast.com.au|date=|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref> He was named to make his [[National Rugby League]] debut for the Gold Coast against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] on 5 May 2013.<ref>{{cite web|author=Chris Barclay|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/the-press/sport/rugby-league/8615157/Ioane-happy-to-have-his-career-back-on-track|title=Ioane happy to have his career back on track|publisher=Stuff.co.nz|date=2013-05-01|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/mark-ioane-to-debut-as-gold-coast-titans-ring-changes/story-e6frep7o-1226632371895|title=Mark Ioane to Debut as Gold Coast Titans Ring Changes|publisher=Couriermail.com.au|accessdate=2015-07-09|date=30 April 2013}}</ref>

===St. George Illawarra Dragons===
On 1 July 2015, Ioane joined the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] mid-season for the rest of the year.<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=https://www.dragons.com.au/news/2015/07/01/dragons_add_depth_to.html|title=Add Depth To Squad|publisher=Dragons.com.au|date=30 June 2015|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
On 9 November 2015, he signed one-year contract with English [[Championship (rugby league)|Kingstone Press Championship]] side, [[London Broncos]] starting in 2016.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/ioane-heads-to-london-broncos-19630/#rwZLPp6s2mRJYXrF.97|title=Ioane heads to London Broncos | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=8 November 2015|accessdate=2015-11-09}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
Ioane joined the [[Leigh Centurions]] ahead of the [[2020 RFL Championship season]].  On 28 May 2022, Ioane played for Leigh in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|work=Yorkshire Post|date=29 May 2022|access-date=29 May 2022 }}</ref>

===Keighley Cougars===
Released by Leigh after three seasons, Ioane signed for Keighley in October 2022 on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web |last1=McAllister |first1=Josh |title=Keighley continue recruitment drive with Mark Ioane signing |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/keighley-continue-recruitment-drive-with-mark-ioane-signing/ |website=LoveRugbyLeague |access-date=12 October 2022 |date=12 October 2022}}</ref>

On 8 January 2026 it was reported that he had left [[Keighley Cougars]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Jan 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former NRL and Super League ace departs Championship club on eve of season|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/mark-ioane-departs-keighley-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>

===North Wales Crusaders===
On 12 January 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[North Wales Crusaders]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=12 Jan 2026|author=Doug Thomson|title=Ex-Bradford Bulls and Keighley Cougars forwards join North Wales Crusaders|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-bradford-bulls-forward-returns-to-game-with-north-wales-crusaders/}}</ref>

===Rochdale Hornets===
On 4 June 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship]] until the end of the 2026 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=4 Jun 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Ex-NRL and Super League forward Mark Ioane finds new home in Championship|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/mark-ioane-transfer-03-06-2026}}</ref>
